title: Jazzmeia Horn at Malta Jazz Festival
subTitle: Vocalist Jazzmeia Horn's 2019 Malta Jazz Festival appearance; a celebration of jazz's future, infused with the soul of its past.

title: North Sea Jazz
subTitle: In 1979, the big band leader and pianist Count Basie brought his famous big band to the North Sea Jazz Festival in The Hague to perform two sets.

title: Miles Davis: Jazz Greats
subTitle: American trumpeter Miles Davis pioneered bebop, cool jazz, hardbop, modal jazz, and electronic music; on October 11, 1964, he performed in Milan with tenor saxophonist Wayne Shorter, pianist Herbie Hancock, bassist Ron Carter and drummer Tony Williams.

title: North Sea Jazz
subTitle: North Sea Jazz

title: Estival Jazz Lugano
subTitle: In 1986, American pianist and composer Keith Jarrett is joined by bassist Gary Peacock and drummer Jack DeJohnette at Estival Jazz Lugano; known as the Standards Trio, they put on an enthralling performance.

title: Christian McBride Quartet at Malta 2018
subTitle: Double bassist Christian McBride presents his band New Jawn at the Malta Jazz Festival on July 20, 2018; hailing from Philadelphia, this musician combines jazz, R&B, pop/rock, hip hop/neo-soul, and classical.

title: Dhafer Youssef -- Jazz à Vienne 2018
subTitle: In 2018, Tunisian composer Dhafer Youssef alternated between his voice and oud playing at Jazz à Vienne; his abilities have to be heard to be believed; he is joined by bassist Matt Brewer and drummer Aaron Parks.

title: Chucho Valdes at Bata Jazz Malta 2019
subTitle: Cuban pianist Chucho Valdés brings his band Jazz Batá Two to the Malta Jazz Festival on July 19, 2019; they deliver a performance that reverberates through the historic island.

title: Live in Australia - Louis Armstrong, Billy Kyle, Trummy Young & Aervell Shaw
subTitle: In 1964 Louis Armstrong recorded his biggest-selling record, Hello, Dolly! He made assorted television appearances, especially in the 1950s and 1960s - the recorded film was a TV Show in Australia when Armstrong was at the peak of his career.

title: Kenny Barron at Lviv Ukraine 2019
subTitle: Pianist Kenny Barron's appearance at the Alfa Jazz Festival in Ukraine's Lviv is a celebration of jazz virtuosity.

title: Astrud Gilberto
subTitle: In 1982, Dutch big band The Skymasters accompanied monumental jazz trumpet legend Dizzy Gillespie and the Brazilian bossa nova singer Astrud Gilberto at the North Sea Jazz Festival in The Hague.

title: Just in Time
subTitle: Experience Tony Bennett in London's Royal Albert Hall on Mar. 2, 1974; the performance showcases Bennett's vocal prowess and charisma.

title: Buck Clayton
subTitle: Trumpeter Buck Clayton is joined by pianist Henri Chaix, double bassist Théo Siegrist, and drummer Pierre Bourru.

title: Miles Davis: Jazz Greats
subTitle: American trumpeter Miles Davis pioneered bebop, cool jazz, hardbop, modal jazz, and electronic music; on October 11, 1964, he performed in Milan with tenor saxophonist Wayne Shorter, pianist Herbie Hancock, bassist Ron Carter and drummer Tony Williams.

title: Stingray Djazz
subTitle: This selection of performances by some of the most influential jazz guitarists in history showcases their unique essence.

title: Jason Moran and Robert Glasper Duet At La Gaité Lyrique, Blue Note Festival
subTitle: As part of the Blue Note Jazz Festival and Blue Note Records' 75th anniversary, pianists Robert Glasper and Jason Moran deliver a jazz piano duet, resulting in an exploration of the history of jazz.

title: Stingray Djazz
subTitle: In 2019, the city of Sofia, Bulgaria, hosted the International A to JazZ Festival; among the featured acts is the jazz trumpeter Christian Scott aTunde Adjuah.

title: Raul Midon at Singapore 2017
subTitle: Raul Midón appears at 2017's Sing Jazz Festival in Singapore with double bassist Romeir Mendez and drummer Billy Williams.

title: Bitches Brew Beyond
subTitle: Trumpeter Wallace Roney revives Miles Davis' legendary album, Bitches Brew. In this project - a brainchild of Miles' family - Miles' nephew takes part. This concert at Vienne's ancient theater in France is a grand tribute to Miles.

title: Dhafer Youssef -- Jazz à Vienne 2018
subTitle: In 2018, Tunisian composer Dhafer Youssef alternated between his voice and oud playing at Jazz à Vienne; his abilities have to be heard to be believed; he is joined by bassist Matt Brewer and drummer Aaron Parks.

title: Kenny Barron at Lviv Ukraine 2019
subTitle: Pianist Kenny Barron's appearance at the Alfa Jazz Festival in Ukraine's Lviv is a celebration of jazz virtuosity.

title: Jacques Loussier in Lepzig: Play Bach and More
subTitle: On July 28th 2004, the day of Bach's 254th anniversary of death, the Jacques Loussier Trio perform a special concert at the famous St. Thomas Church of Leipzig.

title: Fannie Mae
subTitle: Jaco Pastorius, a true musical innovator, takes the stage at the Montreal Jazz Festival; he is transforming the electric bass into a formidable solo instrument, wowing the audience in Canada with his unique style and talent.

title: Rhoda Scott & La Velle: Jazz à Vienne
subTitle: Although American soul and jazz organist Rhoda Scott spent most of her career in France, this performance at the antique theatre of Vienne is special: she presents an intimate and beautiful performance with US gospel and blues singer La Velle.

title: Hackensack
subTitle: Explore the music of John Coltrane; immerse yourself in the collection of performances from the early 1960s that showcase Coltrane's artistry.

title: The Maynard Ferguson Big Band
subTitle: The Maynard Ferguson Big Band's Winter Tour of 1980 included a stop at the Brussels Jazz Club; Ferguson and his band perform some hits of the day.

title: North Sea Jazz
subTitle: In 1982, American jazz fusion band Spyro Gyra took the stage at the North Sea Jazz Festival in The Hague; having struck gold with their album "Morning Dance" just a few years prior, they played a slick set with exuberant energy.

title: Dhafer Youssef -- Jazz à Vienne 2018
subTitle: In 2018, Tunisian composer Dhafer Youssef alternated between his voice and oud playing at Jazz à Vienne; his abilities have to be heard to be believed; he is joined by bassist Matt Brewer and drummer Aaron Parks.

subTitle: Pianist Kenny Barron's appearance at the Alfa Jazz Festival in Ukraine's Lviv is a celebration of jazz virtuosity.

title: The Great Charlie Galbraith All Star Jazz Band
subTitle: In 1963, the small Belgian town of Comblain-la-Tour hosted the talented groups of Charlie Galbraith, Knut Kiesewetter, Bud Shank and Pim Jacobs and Marc Laferrière in a jazz festival that mixed new and old styles.

title: The Great Charlie Galbraith All Star Jazz Band
subTitle: In 1963, the small Belgian town of Comblain-la-Tour hosted the talented groups of Charlie Galbraith, Knut Kiesewetter, Bud Shank and Pim Jacobs and Marc Laferrière in a jazz festival that mixed new and old styles.

subTitle: In 2019, the city of Sofia, Bulgaria, hosts the International A to JazZ Festival; among the featured acts is the jazz trumpeter Christian Scott aTunde Adjuah.

title: Live at Estival Lugano - Franco Ambrosetti, George Gruntz, Steve Coleman & J.F. Clark
subTitle: Since 1977, Estival is a summer jazz festival in Lugano, Switzerland; Estival offers a thrilling and particularly surprising line-up that explores the rich world of contemporary music whilst promoting tolerance, and co-existence.

title: North Sea Jazz
subTitle: In 1986, the third version of jazz fusion band Mahavishnu Orchestra, comprised of guitarist John McLaughlin, pianist Jim Beard, bassist Jonas Hellborg, drummer Danny Gottlieb and saxophonist Bill Evans, played at the North Sea Jazz Festival.

title: The Blue Note Masters -- Ron Carter, Bobby Hutcherson, Javon Jackson & Joe Chambers
subTitle: Explore how Ron Carter, Bobby Hutcherson and Joe Chambers establish themselves as forward-thinking and inventive band leaders, composers and studio musicians.

title: Earl Hines Quartet: Spa, 1977
subTitle: Earl Hines and his group, consisting of Rudy Rutherford, Jimmy Leary, and Eddie Graham perform in Spa in 1977; singer Marva Josie joins the group for two songs before the set closes.

title: McCoy Tyner Trio Live at Estival Lugano
subTitle: McCoy Tyner is an influential jazz pianist from the 20th century, an honor he earned both with Coltrane and in his years of performing following Coltrane's death.

title: Chucho Valdes at Bata Jazz Malta 2019
subTitle: Cuban pianist Chucho Valdés brings his band Jazz Batá Two to the Malta Jazz Festival on July 19, 2019; they deliver a performance that reverberates through the historic island.

title: North Sea Jazz
subTitle: In 1989, American saxophonist Michael Brecker played at the North Sea Jazz Festival in The Hague. Joined by bassist Jeff Andrews, drummer Adam Nussbaum, pianist Joey Calderazzo, and guitarist Mike Stern, Brecker displayed his virtuosity in spades.

title: Avishai Cohen at Lviv Ukraine 2017
subTitle: Avishai Cohen's trio with Noam David and Omri Mor join forces with the International Symphony Orchestra INSO-Lviv, conducted by Christian Schumann.

title: Bobby McFerrin - Estival Jazz Lugano
subTitle: In 1985, American vocal virtuoso Bobby McFerrin takes the stage solo at Estival Jazz Lugano.

subTitle: Pianist Kenny Barron's appearance at the Alfa Jazz Festival in Ukraine's Lviv is a celebration of jazz virtuosity.

title: Jazz à Ramatuelle
subTitle: China Moses takes the stage at Ramatuelle in 2016.

subTitle: Earl Hines and his group, consisting of Rudy Rutherford, Jimmy Leary, and Eddie Graham perform in Spa in 1977; singer Marva Josie joins the group for two songs before the set closes.

title: Aki Rissanen Trio at Jazzclub Unterfahrt, Munich
subTitle: Live from 'Jazzclub Unterfahrt' in Munich, Germany, Finnish pianist Aki Rissanen performs his original compositions; together with Antti Lotjonen on bass and Teppo Makynen on drums, Aki Rissanen Trio showcases the best of the Nordic Tone.

title: Live in Belgium: Paul Desmond, Dave Brubeck & Joe Morello
subTitle: A filmed concert from one prestigious jazz quartet: Paul Desmond, alto sax; Joe Morello, drums; Eugene Wright, bass; and Dave Brubeck, piano, an award-winning jazz supergroup.

title: Maceo Parker: Jazz à Juan
subTitle: At 25, saxophonist Maceo Parker played in James Brown's band, but today he is also known for his collaborations with George Clinton's Parliament-Funkadelic and Bootsy Collins' Rubber Band.

title: Duke Ellington and His Orchestra
subTitle: Duke Ellington, the foremost jazz composer and bandleader, returns to the festival where he revived his career in 1956, performing in Brussels in 1973.

title: Live in Sweden 1963 - Anita Oday, Göran Engdahl, Roman Dylag & John Poole
subTitle: Anita Oday, Göran Engdahl, Roman Dylag and John Poole perform live.

title: Lalah Hathaway
subTitle: Winner of five Grammys in four consecutive years, including the Grammy for best R&B album in 2017, Lalah Hathaway is a force impossible to ignore; see it transform the 2018 Sing Jazz festival scene into a real R&B club.

title: 'The Divine One' in 1958 - Sarah Vaughan, Richard Davis, Ronnell Bright and Art Morgan
subTitle: Renditions of songs by Harold Arlen, Leonard Bernstein, Johnny Burke, the Gershwins, and Stephen Sondheim are pure diva magic.

title: 'The Divine One' in 1958 - Sarah Vaughan, Richard Davis, Ronnell Bright and Art Morgan
subTitle: Renditions of songs by Harold Arlen, Leonard Bernstein, Johnny Burke, the Gershwins, and Stephen Sondheim are pure diva magic.

subTitle: Vocalist Jazzmeia Horn's 2019 Malta Jazz Festival appearance; a celebration of jazz's future, infused with the soul of its past.

title: The Story of Jazz
subTitle: Performances, commentary and compelling historic insights provide an overview of the cross-cultural influences that produced the constantly evolving sounds of jazz music.

title: Swinging Herd Live in England 1964
subTitle: Featuring a blistering one-hour concert from 1964, showcasing one of the hot line-ups of the Swinging Herd.

title: Wynton Marsalis: In This House, on This Morning
subTitle: The final concert of the Münchner Klaviersommer 1992 was by Wynton Marsalis and his septet; the 31-year-old trumpeter performed one of his own jazz suites to the sold-out Philharmonie, under the title of "In This House, On This Morning."

title: Dizzy Gillespie - Jazz Greats
subTitle: American jazz trumpeter Dizzy Gillespie was an influential figure of bebop known for fusing Cuban, Latin American, and Carribean influences in his music.

title: Sammy Price and his New Negro All Stars
subTitle: Texan pianist Sammy Price (1908-1992) played everything from blues and boogie-woogie to swing and jump-blues. Starting in Dallas as a singer and dancer in Alphonso Trent's orchestra, he went to Kansas to form the Texas Bluesicians.
